passage_title: What destroyed the civilisation of Easter Island?

sentences.sentence: Easter Island, or Rapa Nui as it is known locally, is home to several hundred ancient human statues - the moai.
sentences.translation: 复活节岛，当地称为拉帕努伊，是数百尊古代人像——摩艾石像——的所在地。

structure.text: or Rapa Nui as it is known locally
is home to several hundred ancient human statues

difficult_points: or Rapa Nui 补充说明 Easter Island 的当地名称，其中 as 引导方式状语从句。
破折号后的 the moai 是 ancient human statues 的同位语，点明这些人像的名称。

sentences.sentence: After this remote Pacific island was settled by the Polynesians, it remained isolated for centuries.
sentences.translation: 这座偏远的太平洋岛屿被波利尼西亚人定居后，数百年间一直与世隔绝。

structure.text: After this remote Pacific island was settled by the Polynesians

difficult_points: After 引导时间状语从句，从句使用一般过去时的被动语态。
remained isolated 是系表结构，强调长期处于隔绝状态。

expressions.meaning_zh: 一直处于与世隔绝的状态

sentences.sentence: All the energy and resources that went into the moai - some of which are ten metres tall and weigh over 7,000 kilos - came from the island itself.
sentences.translation: 建造摩艾石像所投入的全部能源和资源——其中一些石像高达十米、重逾7000公斤——都来自岛上。

structure.text: some of which are ten metres tall and weigh over 7,000 kilos

difficult_points: that went into the moai 修饰 energy and resources，went into 表示“被投入于”。
破折号中的 some of which 引导非限制性定语从句，which 指代 moai。

sentences.sentence: Yet when Dutch explorers landed in 1722, they met a Stone Age culture.
sentences.translation: 然而，当荷兰探险家于1722年登岛时，他们遇到的却是一个仍处于石器时代的社会。
difficult_points: Yet 表示与前文建造巨型石像所体现的能力形成反差。
when 引导时间状语从句；culture 在此指具有特定发展阶段的社会。

sentences.sentence: The moai were carved with stone tools, then transported for many kilometres, without the use of animals or wheels, to massive stone platforms.
sentences.translation: 摩艾石像先用石器雕刻而成，随后在不用牲畜或车轮的情况下被运送数公里，抵达巨大的石台。

structure.text: without the use of animals or wheels

difficult_points: were carved 和省略 were 的 transported 构成先后发生的两个被动动作。
without the use of ... 说明运输方式的限制，to ... 则说明运输终点。

sentences.sentence: The identity of the moai builders was in doubt until well into the twentieth century.
sentences.translation: 直到20世纪过去了相当一段时间，摩艾石像建造者的身份仍是个谜。

structure.text: The identity of the moai builders
until well into the twentieth century

difficult_points: be in doubt 表示尚未确定，而不是说身份本身值得怀疑。
well into the twentieth century 表示20世纪开始后相当长的一段时间。

sentences.sentence: Thor Heyerdahl, the Norwegian ethnographer and adventurer, thought the statues had been created by pre-Inca peoples from Peru.
sentences.translation: 挪威民族志学家兼探险家托尔·海尔达尔认为，这些石像是由来自秘鲁的前印加民族建造的。

structure.text: the statues had been created by pre-Inca peoples from Peru

difficult_points: 逗号间的名词短语是 Thor Heyerdahl 的同位语，说明其身份。
thought 后省略 that；宾语从句用过去完成时的被动语态，表示建造早于“认为”。

sentences.sentence: Bestselling Swiss author Erich von Däniken believed they were built by stranded extraterrestrials.
sentences.translation: 瑞士畅销书作家埃里希·冯·丹尼肯则认为，它们是由滞留在岛上的外星人建造的。

structure.text: Bestselling Swiss author Erich von Däniken
they were built by stranded extraterrestrials

difficult_points: believed 后省略 that，引出其观点内容。
stranded 是过去分词作定语，表示这些外星人被困或滞留于此。

sentences.sentence: Modern science – linguistic, archaeological and genetic evidence - has definitively proved the moai builders were Polynesians, but not how they moved their creations.
sentences.translation: 现代科学——包括语言学、考古学和遗传学证据——已经确凿证明摩艾石像的建造者是波利尼西亚人，却没有证明他们如何移动这些作品。

structure.text: but not how they moved their creations

difficult_points: 破折号中的三类 evidence 具体说明 Modern science 所依据的证据。
but not 后省略 has proved；how 引导的名词性从句表示仍未解决的问题。

sentences.sentence: Local folklore maintains that the statues walked, while researchers have tended to assume the ancestors dragged the statues somehow, using ropes and logs.
sentences.translation: 当地民间传说坚称石像是自己“走”过去的，而研究人员则往往认为，先民以某种方式用绳索和原木拖动了石像。

structure.text: while researchers have tended to assume
the ancestors dragged the statues somehow

difficult_points: while 在此表示两种解释的对比，而非单纯表示同时发生。
using ropes and logs 是现在分词短语，补充说明 dragged 的方式。

sentences.sentence: When the Europeans arrived, Rapa Nui was grassland, with only a few scrawny trees.
sentences.translation: 欧洲人抵达时，拉帕努伊已是一片草原，只有寥寥几棵瘦弱的树。

structure.text: with only a few scrawny trees

difficult_points: When 从句交代观察到岛屿景观的时间节点。
with 短语补充描述草原上树木稀少且长势不良的状态。

sentences.sentence: In the 1970s and 1980s, though, researchers found pollen preserved in lake sediments, which proved the island had been covered in lush palm forests for thousands of years.
sentences.translation: 不过，在20世纪70至80年代，研究人员在湖底沉积物中发现了保存下来的花粉，这证明该岛此前数千年间一直覆盖着茂密的棕榈林。

structure.text: the island had been covered in lush palm forests for thousands of years

difficult_points: preserved in lake sediments 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 pollen。
which 指代发现花粉这一事实；宾语从句的过去完成时表示森林覆盖早于发现。

sentences.sentence: Only after the Polynesians arrived did those forests disappear.
sentences.translation: 直到波利尼西亚人抵达之后，那些森林才消失。
difficult_points: Only 加时间状语置于句首，引起主句部分倒装，助动词 did 提到主语前。
Only after ... 强调森林消失发生在波利尼西亚人到来之后。

sentences.sentence: US scientist Jared Diamond believes that the Rapanui people - descendants of Polynesian settlers - wrecked their own environment.
sentences.translation: 美国科学家贾雷德·戴蒙德认为，拉帕努伊人——波利尼西亚定居者的后代——毁掉了自己的环境。

structure.text: that the Rapanui people - descendants of Polynesian settlers - wrecked their own environment

difficult_points: that 引导 believes 的宾语从句，陈述 Diamond 的核心观点。
破折号中的 descendants ... 是 Rapanui people 的同位语。

sentences.sentence: They had unfortunately settled on an extremely fragile island – dry, cool, and too remote to be properly fertilised by windblown volcanic ash.
sentences.translation: 不幸的是，他们定居在一座生态极其脆弱的岛上——那里干燥、凉爽，而且过于偏远，无法得到风吹而来的火山灰充分滋养。

structure.text: had unfortunately settled on an extremely fragile island
to be properly fertilised by windblown volcanic ash

difficult_points: 过去完成时 had settled 把定居行为置于随后环境恶化之前。
too remote to be ... 是 too ... to ... 结构，表示岛屿偏远到无法获得充分滋养。

sentences.sentence: When the islanders cleared the forests for firewood and farming, the forests didn't grow back.
sentences.translation: 当岛民为获取柴火和开辟农田而砍伐森林后，森林没有重新生长。

structure.text: When the islanders cleared the forests for firewood and farming

difficult_points: When 从句不仅交代时间，也体现砍伐与森林未恢复之间的因果联系。
for firewood and farming 表示砍伐的两个目的；grow back 指被清除后重新长出。

sentences.sentence: As trees became scarce and they could no longer construct wooden canoes for fishing, they ate birds.
sentences.translation: 随着树木日益稀少、他们再也无法建造木舟捕鱼，岛民转而以鸟类为食。

structure.text: As trees became scarce and they could no longer construct wooden canoes for fishing
they could no longer construct wooden canoes for fishing

difficult_points: As 引导原因兼过程状语，内部由 and 连接两个相互关联的变化。
主句与从句中的 they 都指岛民；no longer 表示过去能做而后来不能再做。

sentences.sentence: Soil erosion decreased their crop yields.
sentences.translation: 土壤侵蚀降低了他们的农作物产量。
difficult_points: decrease 在此是及物动词，直接以 crop yields 作宾语。
their 指代岛民，crop yields 指单位土地或整体农业的作物产量。

sentences.sentence: Before Europeans arrived, the Rapanui had descended into civil war and cannibalism, he maintains.
sentences.translation: 他声称，在欧洲人抵达之前，拉帕努伊人已经陷入内战和食人行为。

structure.text: had descended into civil war and cannibalism

difficult_points: had descended 使用过去完成时，强调所谓崩溃早于欧洲人到来。
he maintains 是后置报道语，其中 he 指 Jared Diamond，表明这是其主张。

sentences.sentence: The collapse of their isolated civilisation, Diamond writes, is a 'worst-case scenario for what may lie ahead of us in our own future'.
sentences.translation: 戴蒙德写道，这个孤立文明的崩溃，是“我们自身未来可能面临之事的最坏情景”。

structure.text: The collapse of their isolated civilisation
a 'worst-case scenario for what may lie ahead of us in our own future'
what may lie ahead of us in our own future

difficult_points: Diamond writes 是插入式报道语，不影响主句的主系表结构。
what 引导名词性从句作 for 的宾语，what 本身相当于“可能发生的事情”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 在前方等待；将要发生于

sentences.sentence: The moai, he thinks, accelerated the self-destruction.
sentences.translation: 他认为，摩艾石像加速了这种自我毁灭。
difficult_points: he thinks 是插入语，he 仍指 Jared Diamond。
the self-destruction 承接上一段所描述的环境破坏与社会崩溃。

sentences.sentence: Diamond interprets them as power displays by rival chieftains who, trapped on a remote little island, lacked other ways of asserting their dominance.
sentences.translation: 戴蒙德把它们解释为相互竞争的酋长展示权力的手段；这些酋长困在一座偏远小岛上，没有其他办法彰显自己的统治地位。

structure.text: interprets them as power displays by rival chieftains
who, trapped on a remote little island, lacked other ways of asserting their dominance
trapped on a remote little island

difficult_points: interpret A as B 表示“把 A 解读为 B”，them 指摩艾石像。
who 引导定语从句修饰 chieftains；trapped ... 是插入其中的过去分词短语。

sentences.sentence: They competed by building ever bigger figures.
sentences.translation: 他们通过建造越来越大的石像来相互竞争。
difficult_points: They 指上一句的 rival chieftains，而不是普通岛民。
by doing 表示竞争的方式；ever bigger 表示尺寸不断增大。

sentences.sentence: Diamond thinks they laid the moai on wooden sledges, hauled over log rails, but that required both a lot of wood and a lot of people.
sentences.translation: 戴蒙德认为，他们把摩艾石像放在木橇上，沿原木轨道拖行，但这种做法既需要大量木材，也需要大量人力。

structure.text: they laid the moai on wooden sledges
but that required both a lot of wood and a lot of people

difficult_points: thinks 后省略 that；hauled over log rails 是过去分词短语，修饰 moai。
but 后的 that 指代前述用木橇和原木轨道运输石像的整套做法。

sentences.sentence: To feed the people, even more land had to be cleared.
sentences.translation: 为了养活这些人，还必须清理更多土地。
difficult_points: 句首不定式短语表示清理更多土地的目的。
had to be cleared 是情态意义与被动语态结合，表示土地必须被开垦。

sentences.sentence: When the wood was gone and civil war began, the islanders began toppling the moai.
sentences.translation: 当木材耗尽、内战爆发后，岛民开始推倒摩艾石像。

structure.text: When the wood was gone and civil war began
the islanders began toppling the moai

difficult_points: When 从句中由 and 连接木材耗尽和内战开始两个背景事件。
begin 后接动名词 toppling；topple 在此作及物动词，表示推倒石像。

sentences.sentence: By the nineteenth century none were standing.
sentences.translation: 到19世纪时，已没有一尊石像仍然矗立。
difficult_points: By 表示截至某一时间节点，强调此前推倒行为的最终结果。
none 指代 moai；were standing 表示仍保持直立状态。

sentences.sentence: Archaeologists Terry Hunt of the University of Hawaii and Carl Lipo of California State University agree that Easter Island lost its lush forests and that it was an 'ecological catastrophe' - but they believe the islanders themselves weren't to blame.
sentences.translation: 夏威夷大学的考古学家特里·亨特和加州州立大学的卡尔·利波都同意，复活节岛失去了茂密森林，而且这是一场“生态灾难”——但他们认为岛民本身不应为此负责。

structure.text: Archaeologists Terry Hunt of the University of Hawaii and Carl Lipo of California State University
that Easter Island lost its lush forests
and that it was an 'ecological catastrophe'
but they believe the islanders themselves weren't to blame

difficult_points: 两个并列的 that 从句都作 agree 的宾语，分别承认森林消失和生态灾难。
but 引出观点转折；themselves 强调岛民并非罪魁，be to blame 表示应受责备。

sentences.sentence: And the moai certainly weren't.
sentences.translation: 摩艾石像当然也不应为此负责。
difficult_points: weren't 后省略 to blame，需从上一句补全意思。
And 把摩艾石像与上一句的 islanders 并列起来，进一步否定其责任。

sentences.sentence: Archaeological excavations indicate that the Rapanui went to heroic efforts to protect the resources of their wind-lashed, infertile fields.
sentences.translation: 考古发掘表明，拉帕努伊人付出了非凡努力，以保护那些饱受强风侵袭、土地贫瘠的田地中的资源。

structure.text: that the Rapanui went to heroic efforts
to protect the resources of their wind-lashed, infertile fields

difficult_points: that 引导 indicate 的宾语从句，说明考古发现所支持的结论。
to protect ... 是目的不定式；wind-lashed 和 infertile 并列修饰 fields。

sentences.sentence: They built thousands of circular stone windbreaks and gardened inside them, and used broken volcanic rocks to keep the soil moist.
sentences.translation: 他们建造了数千道环形石质防风墙，在墙内耕种，并用碎火山岩保持土壤湿润。

structure.text: built thousands of circular stone windbreaks

difficult_points: built、gardened 和 used 是同一主语 They 的三个并列谓语。
them 指 windbreaks；to keep ... 是目的状语，其中 moist 作 soil 的宾语补足语。

sentences.sentence: In short, Hunt and Lipo argue, the prehistoric Rapanui were pioneers of sustainable farming.
sentences.translation: 总之，亨特和利波认为，史前拉帕努伊人是可持续农业的先驱。
difficult_points: Hunt and Lipo argue 是插入式报道语，主句从 the prehistoric Rapanui 开始。
pioneers of 表示某领域的先驱，概括前文保护土地的各种做法。

sentences.sentence: Hunt and Lipo contend that moai-building was an activity that helped keep the peace between islanders.
sentences.translation: 亨特和利波主张，建造摩艾石像是一项有助于维持岛民之间和平的活动。

structure.text: that helped keep the peace between islanders

difficult_points: 第一个 that 引导 contend 的宾语从句，第二个 that 引导定语从句修饰 activity。
help 后接省略 to 的动词原形 keep；between islanders 表示和平存在于岛民群体之间。

sentences.sentence: They also believe that moving the moai required few people and no wood, because they were walked upright.
sentences.translation: 他们还认为，移动摩艾石像只需要很少的人，而且不需要木材，因为石像是以直立方式被“走”动的。

structure.text: that moving the moai required few people and no wood

difficult_points: 宾语从句中 moving the moai 是动名词短语作主语。
because 从句使用不常见的被动结构 were walked，表示人们让石像直立着移动；they 指 moai。

sentences.sentence: On that issue, Hunt and Lipo say, archaeological evidence backs up Rapanui folklore.
sentences.translation: 亨特和利波表示，在这个问题上，考古证据支持拉帕努伊的民间传说。
difficult_points: Hunt and Lipo say 是插入式报道语，主句主语是 archaeological evidence。
that issue 指石像是否能被直立“走动”；back up 表示证据为说法提供支持。

sentences.sentence: Recent experiments indicate that as few as 18 people could, with three strong ropes and a bit of practice, easily manoeuvre a 1,000 kg moai replica a few hundred metres.
sentences.translation: 近期实验表明，只要三根结实的绳索并稍加练习，少至18个人就能轻松地将一尊1000公斤重的摩艾复制品移动数百米。

structure.text: that as few as 18 people could
with three strong ropes and a bit of practice
easily manoeuvre a 1,000 kg moai replica a few hundred metres

difficult_points: as few as 强调人数之少；could 与被插入的 with 短语分隔，后接 manoeuvre。
a few hundred metres 是距离状语，说明复制品被移动的距离。

sentences.sentence: The figures' fat bellies tilted them forward, and a D-shaped base allowed handlers to roll and rock them side to side.
sentences.translation: 石像凸起的腹部使其向前倾斜，而D形底座让操作者能够将它们左右滚动和摇摆。

structure.text: and a D-shaped base allowed handlers
to roll and rock them side to side

difficult_points: tilt A forward 是“动词 + 宾语 + 方向补语”结构，them 指石像。
allow someone to do 表示使某人能够做某事；roll 和 rock 是并列动词。

sentences.sentence: Moreover, Hunt and Lipo are convinced that the settlers were not wholly responsible for the loss of the island's trees.
sentences.translation: 此外，亨特和利波确信，定居者并不应为岛上树木的消失负全部责任。

structure.text: that the settlers were not wholly responsible for the loss of the island's trees

difficult_points: be convinced that 表示对后面从句陈述的观点确信不疑。
not wholly responsible 是部分否定，意为并非承担全部责任，而非完全没有责任。

sentences.sentence: Archaeological finds of nuts from the extinct Easter Island palm show tiny grooves, made by the teeth of Polynesian rats.
sentences.translation: 出土的已灭绝复活节岛棕榈坚果上有细小沟痕，考古发现表明这些痕迹是波利尼西亚鼠的牙齿造成的。

structure.text: Archaeological finds of nuts from the extinct Easter Island palm
made by the teeth of Polynesian rats

difficult_points: 主语中心词是复数 finds，因此谓语使用 show。
made by ... 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 grooves，指出沟痕的形成者。

sentences.sentence: The rats arrived along with the settlers, and in just a few years, Hunt and Lipo calculate, they would have overrun the island.
sentences.translation: 老鼠随定居者一同到来；亨特和利波推算，仅仅几年之内，它们就会遍布整座岛屿。

structure.text: The rats arrived along with the settlers
and in just a few years
they would have overrun the island

difficult_points: along with 表示老鼠伴随定居者到来，并不构成并列主语。
Hunt and Lipo calculate 是插入语；would have overrun 表示研究者对过去情况的推算。

sentences.sentence: They would have prevented the reseeding of the slow-growing palm trees and thereby doomed Rapa Nui's forest, even without the settlers' campaign of deforestation.
sentences.translation: 它们会阻止生长缓慢的棕榈树重新播种，从而注定拉帕努伊森林走向毁灭，即使没有定居者大规模砍伐森林也是如此。

structure.text: would have prevented the reseeding of the slow-growing palm trees
and thereby doomed Rapa Nui's forest
even without the settlers' campaign of deforestation

difficult_points: They 指 Polynesian rats；would have prevented 延续上一句对过去情形的推断。
thereby 表示前一行为导致后一结果；even without 强调没有人为砍伐也会造成灾难。

expressions.meaning_zh: 大规模砍伐森林的行动

sentences.sentence: No doubt the rats ate birds' eggs too.
sentences.translation: 毫无疑问，老鼠也吃鸟蛋。
difficult_points: No doubt 是评注性状语，表示作者认为这一推断几乎可以确定。
birds' 是复数所有格，表示多只鸟所产的蛋。

sentences.sentence: Hunt and Lipo also see no evidence that Rapanui civilisation collapsed when the palm forest did.
sentences.translation: 亨特和利波也没有发现证据表明，拉帕努伊文明与棕榈林在同一时期崩溃。
difficult_points: that 从句说明 evidence 的具体内容，可理解为同位语从句。
when the palm forest did 中 did 替代 collapsed，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: They think its population grew rapidly and then remained more or less stable until the arrival of the Europeans, who introduced deadly diseases to which islanders had no immunity.
sentences.translation: 他们认为，岛上人口曾迅速增长，随后大体保持稳定，直到欧洲人到来；欧洲人带来了岛民毫无免疫力的致命疾病。

structure.text: its population grew rapidly and then remained more or less stable until the arrival of the Europeans
to which islanders had no immunity

difficult_points: think 后省略 that；宾语从句中 grew 和 remained 是先后发生的并列谓语。
who 修饰 Europeans；to which 修饰 diseases，介词 to 来自 have no immunity to。

sentences.sentence: Then in the nineteenth century slave traders decimated the population, which shrivelled to 111 people by 1877.
sentences.translation: 随后在19世纪，奴隶贩子使人口锐减；到1877年，人口萎缩至仅111人。
difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，指代 population。
shrivelled to 表示人口萎缩到某一数值；by 1877 表示截至该年。

sentences.sentence: Hunt and Lipo's vision, therefore, is one of an island populated by peaceful and ingenious moai builders and careful stewards of the land, rather than by reckless destroyers ruining their own environment and society.
sentences.translation: 因此，在亨特和利波的图景中，岛上居住的是和平而有创造力的摩艾建造者和细心的土地守护者，而不是毁坏自身环境与社会的鲁莽破坏者。

structure.text: populated by peaceful and ingenious moai builders and careful stewards of the land
ruining their own environment and society

difficult_points: one 替代 vision，one of an island 表示一种关于这座岛的图景。
populated by ... 与 rather than by ... 构成对比；ruining ... 修饰 reckless destroyers。

sentences.sentence: 'Rather than a case of abject failure, Rapa Nui is an unlikely story of success', they claim.
sentences.translation: 他们声称：“拉帕努伊并非一个彻底失败的案例，而是一个出人意料的成功故事。”

structure.text: Rather than a case of abject failure
is an unlikely story of success

difficult_points: Rather than ... 将“彻底失败的案例”与后面的“成功故事”直接对照。
unlikely 在此表示这个成功故事出人意料；they claim 是引语后的报道语。

sentences.sentence: Whichever is the case, there are surely some valuable lessons which the world at large can learn from the story of Rapa Nui.
sentences.translation: 无论哪一种说法属实，拉帕努伊的故事无疑都能给整个世界带来一些宝贵教训。

structure.text: there are surely some valuable lessons
which the world at large can learn from the story of Rapa Nui

difficult_points: Whichever 引导让步状语从句，表示无论两种解释中的哪一种成立。
which 引导定语从句修饰 lessons，并在 learn from 的语义关系中充当宾语。
